name: setup-btc-investor
description: Mid-career Bitcoin-conviction investor going through first-time setup
persona: Alex Chen, 42, Austin TX. Software engineer turned investor. 18 BTC total (15 in Coldcard, 3 on Coinbase). $200K in Fidelity index funds. $80K savings. $50K Roth IRA. $1.7M total. $15K/month expenses, $25K/month consulting income.

criteria: Automatically starts onboarding/setup process without being asked
Welcomes the user warmly
Asks about the user's identity or life situation
turns.description: Should auto-detect empty profile and start /setup

turns.user: I'm Alex Chen, 42, living in Austin, Texas. Software engineer turned investor. Married, two kids (8 and 12). Monthly income from consulting about $25K, expenses around $15K.
criteria: Acknowledges the user by name (Alex)
Transitions to asking about goals or financial objectives
Does NOT jump straight to portfolio details yet
turns.description: Should capture identity and transition to goals

turns.user: Three goals: 1) Financial independence by 50. 2) Buy a house in Austin, $800K-1M budget, within 2 years. 3) College fund for both kids, $200K each over 10 years.
criteria: Acknowledges all three goals specifically
Transitions to asking about current portfolio or holdings
May provide initial analysis on goal feasibility
turns.description: Should capture goals and ask about portfolio

turns.user: 15 BTC in a Coldcard hardware wallet. 3 BTC on Coinbase for trading. $200K in Fidelity brokerage (index funds). $80K in savings. $50K Roth IRA. Total about $1.7M at BTC $90K.
criteria: Identifies the distinct accounts/entities (hardware wallet, exchange, brokerage, savings, IRA)
Notes the high BTC concentration (90%+ of portfolio)
Recognizes the self-custody vs exchange split
turns.description: Should identify entities and begin creating them

turns.user: Monthly expenses $15K. The $80K in savings is my liquid reserve. I also get $25K/month from consulting.
criteria: Calculates or discusses runway (liquid reserves / monthly burn)
Acknowledges the income surplus ($25K income - $15K expenses = $10K/month)
References runway in months

turns.user: I agree with the bitcoin-fo beliefs mostly. No leverage — yes. Self-custody — yes. But I'm comfortable at 60-70% BTC concentration, not 80%+.
criteria: Acknowledges the user's agreement with pack beliefs
Records the 60-70% concentration preference as a user override or disagreement
Does not push back against the user's preference
turns.description: Should capture disagreement as user override

turns.user: Buckets look good. Safety Floor = savings. Core Conviction = BTC. Growth Engine = Fidelity. No moonshots yet. Lifestyle Fund for the house downpayment.
criteria: Maps the user's holdings to the bucket structure
Acknowledges the Safety Floor, Core Conviction, Growth Engine, and Lifestyle Fund
Confirms or summarizes the bucket allocation

turns.user: If BTC dropped 60%? I'd hold. Rode through 2022. What worries me is losing consulting income AND a crash simultaneously. Never panic-sold.
criteria: Captures the revealed risk preference (high conviction holder)
Identifies the dual-risk scenario (income loss + crash) as the real concern
Does NOT label the user with a risk category (conservative/moderate/aggressive)
turns.description: Should capture revealed preferences without labels

turns.user: Min runway 6 months is fine since I have income. Max custodian 30% good. BTC concentration upper comfort at 70%. I have a will but no POA or digital asset plan.
criteria: Records the guardrail values (6 months runway, 30% custodian, 70% concentration)
Flags the missing POA and digital asset plan as gaps
Summarizes the guardrail calibration

turns.user: Concise communication, be direct. What's my status?
criteria: Acknowledges the communication preference
Signals that setup is complete or nearly complete
Suggests running /start or the daily snapshot as next step
Provides a brief portfolio status summary
turns.description: Should set preferences and show completion + next steps
